context_note: Use your existing project context window. Do NOT re-scaffold the project. All changes must extend the current codebase, match the existing dark-navy / purple theme (primary: #2D2B6B, accent: #6C63FF), and preserve all existing student-facing pages.

reference_screenshot.description: The uploaded screenshot shows the AWS Cloud Architecture & DevOps course page with a highlighted enrollment card (₹3,499 price, Enroll Now CTA, 12 lessons, 7 hours, certificate, AI progress tracking). This is the student view that must remain intact and become data-driven from the instructor's course entries.
key_ui_elements: Course hero with title, subtitle, star rating, student count, hours, level badge
Enrollment card (price, Enroll Now button, course meta-list)
About This Course section below the fold

tasks.title: Database Schema – Instructors & Course Ownership
tasks.description: Extend the existing database (use whatever DB layer is already in the project – do not swap it out). Add or migrate the following relationships so the rest of the tasks have a stable data contract.

schema_changes.note: Link every existing instructor user record to this table on migration.
The AWS Cloud Architecture & DevOps course shown in the screenshot must be seeded here and linked to the logged-in instructor (Sarah Jones).

acceptance_criteria: All existing course data is preserved and backfilled with instructor_id.
Seed script creates at least one instructor (Sarah Jones) owning the AWS course.
Enrollments table is ready for the Enroll Now button (T5).

tasks.title: Instructor Authentication Guard & Role Check
tasks.description: Ensure the route /instructor/* is protected. When a user with role='instructor' logs in, redirect them to /instructor/dashboard instead of the student home. Non-instructors hitting /instructor/* must be redirected to the login page with a toast: 'Instructor access only'.
implementation_notes: Reuse the existing auth context/hook – do not introduce a second auth system.
Add a role field to the user session payload if not already present.
Sarah Jones' account must have role='instructor' in the seed.
acceptance_criteria: Logging in as Sarah Jones lands on /instructor/dashboard.
Logging in as a student and manually navigating to /instructor/dashboard redirects to login.
JWT or session cookie correctly carries the role.

tasks.title: Instructor Dashboard – Overview Page (/instructor/dashboard)
tasks.description: Build the main instructor landing page after login. Style it to match the dark-navy (#1E1B4B / #2D2B6B) background and purple accent (#6C63FF) used throughout the project.

layout_sections.content: Instructor avatar, name ('Welcome back, Sarah Jones'), role badge. Same top-nav as the rest of the site but with instructor-specific links: Dashboard | My Courses | Analytics | Settings.

cards: Total Revenue (sum of enrollments × course price)
layout_sections.note: Cards use the same glassmorphism / rounded card style from the existing UI.
The AWS Cloud Architecture & DevOps course must appear here on first load (from seed data).

acceptance_criteria: Stats are computed live from the database – no hardcoded numbers.
My Courses table renders the seeded AWS course.
Clicking 'Manage Sections & Videos' opens T4 flow.
Toggle Publish changes is_published and immediately reflects in the student course listing.

tasks.title: Course Builder – Sections & Video Lessons Manager
tasks.description: Create the course content editor reachable from the dashboard 'Manage Sections & Videos' action. This is the core instructor workflow.

ui_components.note: Pre-filled from DB. Save button POSTs to PATCH /api/instructor/courses/:id.

behaviour: Drag-and-drop re-ordering of sections (use existing DnD library if present, else a simple up/down arrow approach is acceptable for the demo).
Each section is an accordion showing its lessons.
Add Section button appends a new section row with an inline text input for the title.
Inside each section, Add Lesson button opens a modal/drawer with fields: Lesson Title, Video URL (YouTube/Vimeo/direct), Duration (minutes), Free Preview toggle.
Existing lessons show a small play icon and the video URL as a clickable link.
Delete icons on both sections and lessons with a confirmation prompt.

api_endpoints_to_implement: GET    /api/instructor/courses/:id/curriculum  → returns sections[] with nested lessons[]
acceptance_criteria: Instructor can add a section titled 'Introduction to AWS' and save it.
Inside that section, instructor can add a lesson 'What is EC2?' with a YouTube URL and it persists on page refresh.
The student course detail page (see T6) renders those same lessons dynamically.

tasks.title: Enroll Now Button – Functional Enrollment Flow
tasks.description: Wire up the 'Enroll Now' button visible in the screenshot on the AWS course card (price ₹3,499). The button is currently static; make it functional.
flow: If not logged in → redirect to /login with return URL preserved.

If logged in but not enrolled → show a confirmation modal: 'Enroll in AWS Cloud Architecture & DevOps for ₹3,499?' with Confirm and Cancel buttons. (Payment processing is out of scope; simulate with a direct DB insert.)
On Confirm → POST /api/enrollments { courseId } → insert into enrollments table → show success toast 'You're enrolled! Start learning.' → button changes to 'Go to Course' (green).
If already enrolled → button renders as 'Go to Course' on initial load.
Instructor viewing their own course → button renders as 'Edit Course' linking to /instructor/courses/:id/curriculum.
api_endpoints_to_implement: POST   /api/enrollments           → enroll current user in a course
acceptance_criteria: A student account can enroll in the AWS course and the enrollment persists in the DB.
Post-enrollment the dashboard student count on the instructor dashboard increments.
Sarah Jones sees 'Edit Course' instead of 'Enroll Now' on the same page.

tasks.title: Student Course Detail Page – Dynamic Curriculum Display
tasks.description: Update the existing student-facing course detail page (the page shown in the screenshot) so the curriculum/lessons section below 'About This Course' is populated from the database instead of being static.
changes: Fetch GET /api/courses/:id/curriculum (public endpoint, no auth required for published courses).
Render an accordion per section. Each lesson row shows: lesson index, title, duration, Free Preview badge (if applicable), and a Watch button.
Watch button: if enrolled (or free preview) → open video in a modal/iframe player using the video_url stored by the instructor. If not enrolled → show 'Enroll to Watch' tooltip.
Lesson count and total hours in the enrollment card (12 lessons, 7 hours in screenshot) must be computed dynamically from the DB.
acceptance_criteria: Any lesson the instructor adds via T4 appears on this page without a redeploy.
Enrolled student can click Watch and see the video URL open (iframe or new tab).
The stat '12 lessons' in the card matches the actual lesson count in the DB.

tasks.title: Add New Course Flow for Instructors
tasks.description: The '+ Add New Course' button on the instructor dashboard should open a multi-step form (or a single-page form is acceptable for the demo) to create a brand-new course.
form_fields: Category (dropdown matching existing category tags in the project)
Full Description (rich text or plain textarea)
on_submit: Redirect to /instructor/courses/:newId/curriculum so the instructor can immediately add sections and lessons.
Show toast 'Course created as Draft. Add your curriculum now.'
acceptance_criteria: New course appears in the My Courses table as 'Draft'.
Instructor can then add sections/lessons via T4.
Course does NOT appear in the public student listing until Toggle Publish is hit.

tasks.title: Instructor–Student Data Bridge: Live Stats & Connectivity Validation
tasks.description: This task ensures the end-to-end data flow is demonstrable and correct. It is primarily a wiring / integration task, not new UI.
checklist: When an instructor publishes a course (is_published=true), it immediately appears in the student /courses listing page.
When a student enrolls, the instructor's dashboard stats card 'Total Students Enrolled' reflects the new count on next load (no manual refresh needed beyond normal page navigation).
The instructor's 'My Courses' table revenue column is computed as: SUM(enrollments for that course) × course.price.
The student's /dashboard (if it exists) shows enrolled courses with progress_percent from the enrollments table.
All API routes under /api/instructor/* enforce that the calling user's instructor_id matches the resource being modified (ownership check) – return 403 otherwise.
acceptance_criteria: Demo script: (1) Login as Sarah Jones → create course → add section + lesson → publish → logout → login as student → enroll → watch lesson → logout → login as Sarah Jones → verify student count increased.
The full demo script completes without errors in the browser console.

tasks.title: UI Polish – Match Existing Theme Exactly
tasks.description: All new instructor pages must visually match the LearnHub design system visible in the screenshot: dark navy background, purple gradient accents, white card surfaces, rounded-2xl corners, the same font stack, and the existing nav bar component.
specifics: Reuse existing Button, Card, Badge, Modal, and Toast components from the current component library.
Do NOT introduce a new CSS framework or component library.
Instructor-only nav items should be visually distinct (e.g., a subtle 'Instructor Mode' chip next to the logo) so the role context is clear during the demo.
All form inputs must use the same input style (dark bg, purple focus ring) already present in the login/register forms.

demo_script.description: Use this to validate the full implementation before marking complete.
steps: 1. Run migrations / seed. Confirm instructors, courses, sections, lessons tables exist and AWS course is seeded under Sarah Jones.
2. Log in as Sarah Jones (instructor). Confirm redirect to /instructor/dashboard.
3. Verify the AWS course appears in My Courses table with correct stats.
4. Click 'Manage Sections & Videos' for the AWS course. Add a section 'Module 1: AWS Fundamentals' and a lesson 'Intro to EC2' with URL https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=example.
5. Go to the student-facing course page (/courses/:awsId). Confirm 'Module 1: AWS Fundamentals' and 'Intro to EC2' are visible in the curriculum section.
6. Log out. Log in as a student account.
7. Navigate to the AWS course page. Click 'Enroll Now'. Confirm enrollment modal. Confirm enrollment.
8. Verify button changes to 'Go to Course'. Click 'Intro to EC2' → Watch. Video URL should open.
9. Log out. Log in as Sarah Jones. Verify student count on dashboard increased by 1.
10. Use '+ Add New Course' to create a new draft course. Confirm it appears as Draft. Toggle Publish. Confirm it appears in student /courses listing.
